target.title: fix(memory-core): skip qmd zero-hit search sync
target.body:
## What changed

Fixes #90023.

`memory_search` no longer runs the synchronous zero-hit forced sync path when the active memory backend is `qmd`. Non-QMD backends keep the existing zero-hit repair behavior.

## Behavior proof

Setup:

```text
Repository: openclaw/openclaw
Base: origin/main at 2b31ad2e
Worktree: /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90023
Implementation path: Fastino Code TUI, DeepSeek V4 Flash Free, XML+SS, SLM lane, max
```

Before:

```ts
if (rawResults.length === 0 && activeMemory.manager.sync) {
  await activeMemory.manager.sync({ reason: "search", force: true });
  rawResults = await activeMemory.manager.search(query, searchOptions);
}
```

For a QMD-backed zero-hit search, that condition allowed a synchronous forced `manager.sync({ reason: "search", force: true })` inside the interactive `memory_search` path.

After:

```ts
if (
  rawResults.length === 0 &&
  activeMemory.manager.sync &&
  statusBeforeRetry.backend !== "qmd"
) {
  await activeMemory.manager.sync({ reason: "search", force: true });
  rawResults = await activeMemory.manager.search(query, searchOptions);
}
```

The new regression test sets the memory backend to `qmd`, returns zero hits, and asserts:

```text
results == []
searchCalls == 1
getMemorySyncMockCalls() == 0
```

After-fix real QMD CLI proof:

```text
Repository: openclaw/openclaw
Worktree: /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90023
QMD binary: /opt/homebrew/bin/qmd
OpenClaw state/config: isolated under /private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030
Config: memory.backend = "qmd", memory.qmd.searchMode = "search"
Sync disabled for the zero-hit timing probe:
  agents.defaults.memorySearch.sync.onSessionStart = false
  agents.defaults.memorySearch.sync.onSearch = false

$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/state \
  pnpm --dir /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90023 \
  openclaw config validate
Config valid: /private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/openclaw.json

$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/state \
  pnpm --dir /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90023 \
  openclaw memory status --json --agent proof-agent
status.backend = "qmd"
status.provider = "qmd"
status.files = 2
status.chunks = 2
status.vector.enabled = false
audit.qmd.dbBytes = 98304

$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/state \
  pnpm --dir /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90023 \
  openclaw memory search --query no-such-token-90030 --max-results 5 --json --agent proof-agent
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "proof-agent" mode=cli collections=2 durationMs=128
{
  "results": []
}
elapsed_seconds=3.335
```

The zero-hit command output shows QMD backend initialization and an empty result set, with no `qmd sync completed` / forced update log during the search command. That is the expected after-fix behavior: the QMD zero-hit `memory_search` path returns without running the synchronous forced `manager.sync({ reason: "search", force: true })` fallback.

## How to test

```sh
pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results:

```text
pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)

pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       34 passed (34)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check
No output.
```

## Platforms tested

- macOS local worktree
- Node/Vitest through the repository `pnpm test` wrapper

## Limitations

The live proof uses an isolated local QMD setup with BM25 `search` mode and redacted temporary paths. It verifies the QMD zero-hit path does not trigger the synchronous forced update fallback; it does not measure a large real-world `qmd update` delay.


## Additional MCP tool-path proof

```text
Repository: openclaw/openclaw
Worktree: /Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90023
MCP server: node --import tsx src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.ts
Tool invoked: memory_search through MCP callTool, not openclaw memory search CLI
Config/state: isolated under /private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030
Default agent workspace: /private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/state/workspace-proof-agent
Query: no-such-token-90030

$ NODE_PATH=/Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-90023/node_modules \
  node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-qmd-proof-90030/call-memory-search-mcp.ts
tools_has_memory_search=true
elapsed_seconds=104.050
tool_result_json=
{
  "content": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "{\n  \"results\": [],\n  \"provider\": \"qmd\",\n  \"model\": \"qmd\",\n  \"citations\": \"auto\",\n  \"debug\": {\n    \"backend\": \"qmd\",\n    \"searchMs\": 2,\n    \"hits\": 0\n  }\n}"
    }
  ]
}
mcp_stderr=
[memory] qmd search denied by scope (channel=unknown, chatType=unknown, session=<none>)
```

This proof exercises the changed `memory_search` plugin tool entry point through the MCP tool server. The result payload reports `provider: "qmd"`, `debug.backend: "qmd"`, `debug.searchMs: 2`, and zero hits. Stderr contains the expected no-session scope warning from this standalone MCP invocation, but it does not contain `qmd sync completed` or a forced `qmd update` log during the zero-hit tool call. The 104 second wall time is cold standalone MCP/plugin startup; the QMD search itself reports 2 ms in the tool debug payload.
target.author: sahibzada-allahyar
